title: All code should be reachable
description: All code should be reachableAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcweunusedOnce control flow has been moved out of the current code block, any subsequent statements become effectively unreachable.Why is this an issue?More InfoJump statements (return,breakandnext) move control flow out of the current code block. So any statements that come after a jump are dead code.Noncompliant code exampledef foo(a) i = 10 return a + i # Noncompliant i += 1 # dead code endCompliant solutiondef foo(a) i = 10 return a + i endAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
# Noncompliant: Code after return is unreachable
def calculate_total(price, tax)
  return price + tax
  puts 'This will never execute'  # Dead code
  price * 0.1  # Dead code
end

# Noncompliant: Code after break is unreachable
array.each do |item|
  break if item.nil?
  puts 'This will never execute'  # Dead code
end
compliant:
# Compliant: All code is reachable
def calculate_total(price, tax)
  total = price + tax
  puts "Total calculated: #{total}"
  total
end

# Compliant: Proper control flow
array.each do |item|
  next if item.nil?
  puts "Processing: #{item}"
  process_item(item)
end

checks.message: Code after return/break/next statement is unreachable
